# Distribution Portfolio Model

Do not rely on one algorithmic surface.

## Portfolio channels

```text
organic feed
search long-tail
owned audience
community distribution
creator/partner collaborations
paid micro-seeding
profile/pinned content
playlist/series architecture
cross-platform native translations
```

## Channel purpose

| Channel | Purpose | Guarantee type |
|---|---|---|
| Organic feed | upside and discovery | no guarantee |
| Search | long-tail durability | partial, topic-dependent |
| Owned audience | repeat reach | controllable if audience exists |
| Community | targeted feedback | semi-controllable |
| Partner/collab | borrowed trust | controllable through relationships |
| Paid micro-seeding | minimum exposure | controllable by budget |
| Pinned/profile | conversion | controllable asset placement |
| Cross-platform | risk diversification | controllable execution |

## Portfolio allocation

For each 10-video batch:

```text
4 feed-native tests
2 search-native evergreen tutorials
1 sendable identity/social video
1 saveable checklist/template
1 proof/authority teardown
1 experimental format via Trial Reel or controlled exposure
```

## Why this works better

A feed-only strategy dies when the platform does not distribute the post. A portfolio strategy still produces:

```text
search traffic
owned audience learning
community feedback
partner exposure
paid-test data
profile conversion assets
```
